page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.48xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by AWS Graviton processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 192 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 model running at 2.8 GHz. The processor configuration includes 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 96 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps. The server does not include any GPU accelerators.

In benchmark evaluations, the m9gd.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, achieving a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 382,376.39 and a PassMark CPU score of 107,019.88. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 27,785.94 GB/sec and large block uncached read speeds at 729.18 GB/sec. Database operations are highly performant, as evidenced by a PassMark database score of 61,965.11 and Redis SET operations reaching 506.95 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core scoring 2,009.1, and LLM text generation speeds are relatively low.

Given its high core density and integrated high-capacity NVMe storage, the server offers strong resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for scale-out workloads. It is highly suited for high-throughput database deployments, large-scale web serving, multi-threaded data compression, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring high network and local storage performance.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.48xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by 192 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 cores running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the arm64 architecture and the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 768.0 GB of memory alongside 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The server delivers top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, with notable benchmark results including a PassMark CPU score of 107,019.88 and Redis SET operations reaching 506.95 million operations per second. While single-core performance is moderate, its massive local storage and high core density provide excellent resource efficiency for demanding multi-threaded tasks. This instance is optimized for high-throughput database hosting, static web serving, multi-threaded data processing, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.

tagline: An arm64 general-purpose platform featuring 192 dedicated cores, high-capacity local NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-core database performance.

Equipped with 768 GB of system memory and 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Best suited for high-throughput databases, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing
